title: Versioning, Delete Markers, Object Lock, and Recovery · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs versioning, delete markers, object lock, and recovery from browser, Go service, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for version and immutable-retention recovery runbook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.
List versions and delete markers, resolve current reads, restore by removing a marker or copying a version, and preserve audit identity.
Apply governance, compliance, legal hold, retention date, and authorized bypass by version with irreversible-risk review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Restore Drill Evidence
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go file-delivery team handles case 7166 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate version identity, immutable retention meaning, and executed restore evidence; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Run sampled restore drills with authorization, known bytes, metadata, dependencies, time objectives, audit evidence, and safe cleanup.

Inspect the Restore Drill Evidence trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Restore Drill Evidence.

Competency probe: Run sampled restore drills with authorization, known bytes, metadata, dependencies, time objectives, audit evidence, and safe cleanup.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Restore Drill Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Restore Drill Evidence through the concrete version and immutable-retention recovery runbook case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Restore Drill Evidence, keep Restore Drill Evidence connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run sampled restore drills with authorization, known bytes, metadata, dependencies, time objectives, audit evidence, and safe cleanup.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Restore Drill Evidence, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Restore Drill Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Restore Drill Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Go file-delivery team handles case 3737 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate version identity, immutable retention meaning, and executed restore evidence; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Run sampled restore drills with authorization, known bytes, metadata, dependencies, time objectives, audit evidence, and safe cleanup.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Restore Drill Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: A documented restore command proves recovery without executing a drill.
